\noindent In Figure 5.19 we have plotted expectations against the recorded values of revenue, indicating which individual observations fall outside of the 99\% prediction interval. The following code is used to create it.

\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\begingroup
\fvset{listparameters={%
  \setlength{\topsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\partopsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\parsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}%
}}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
# Convert predictions to a data frame and add the date column
pred_df <- data.frame(
  date = USSteamCoHold$date,
  recorded = USSteamCoHold$revenue,
  lwr = predictions[, "lwr"],
  fit = predictions[, "fit"],
  upr = predictions[, "upr"]
)
pred_df$outside = with(pred_df, recorded < lwr | recorded > upr)

expectations_plot <- ggplot(pred_df, aes(x = date)) +
  # Plot the predicted values as a line
  geom_line(aes(y = fit, color = "Expectation"), linewidth = 1) +
  # Add the prediction interval as a shaded area
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lwr, ymax = upr), fill = "#00338D", alpha = 0.2) +
  # Plot the actual values, with conditional coloring for outside interval points
  geom_line(aes(y = recorded, color = "Recorded"), linewidth = 1) +
  # Highlight points outside interval in red
  geom_point(data = subset(pred_df, outside), aes(y = recorded), color = "#E36877", size = 2) +
  # Define color for predicted and actual values
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Expectation" = "#00338D", "Recorded" = "#E36877"),
    name = ""
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Month in 2014",
    y = "Revenue ($)"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "1 month",  # Adjusts to one gridline per month
    date_labels = "%b"  # Optional: shows abbreviated month names
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

expectations_plot
\end{Verbatim}
\endgroup
\par\addvspace{\topsep}
